expertAssessments.rationale: The standard five-question suite passed 4/5, but the network-only follow-up failed by returning a generic system summary instead of respecting prior context and filtering network issues.
expertAssessments.nextAction: After deploying today's NLQ/anomaly routing changes, rerun the standard five-question suite and specifically recheck the network-only follow-up.

expertAssessments.rationale: Dashboard and anomaly/trend data are grounded, but the AI follow-up did not preserve network metric intent and the anomaly/trend UI still presents confidence-style labels.
expertAssessments.nextAction: Verify network metric selection and replace or justify confidence labels in the anomaly/trend surface.

expertAssessments.rationale: The QA used authenticated UI paths only, observed no browser console warnings/errors, and did not bypass CSRF-protected AI endpoints.
Vercel production is healthy but does not contain today's local workspace changes; production remains on v8.11.175 commit 7da68ac4a1 while local HEAD is 73281f363f02 with unstaged changes.
expertAssessments.nextAction: Use the GitLab CI release path or an explicit preview deployment before treating this as validation of today's local changes.

expertAssessments.rationale: Playwright MCP covered route render, AI assistant UI flow, five standard AI prompts, anomaly/trend tab, network request capture, console capture, and durable evidence artifacts.

notes: Direct Playwright MCP initially failed because a stale MCP Chrome profile was locked; the MCP-only Chrome process was terminated and the Playwright MCP session then ran normally.
This run is not release-facing because it checks the current production deployment rather than a deployed copy of today's local workspace changes.
Vercel production network requests included /api/ai/nlq/extract-entities 200 and /api/ai/jobs 201 during the conversational QA.
